Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>[ASK:dream_entry2] ad</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-06</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-07-06T22:19:30.639Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\POLICIES\\\\preamble-frozen-snapshot.md\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\POLICIES\\\\preamble-frozen-snapshot.md\",\"content\":\"# Preamble Frozen-Snapshot Policy\\n\\n**Audience**: anyone editing `src/sessionManager.ts`, `src/memory.ts`,\\nor anything in the path that feeds Kiro / Codex / Claude's first prompt.\\n**Status**: enforced invariant, not an aspiration.\\n**Inspired by**: Hermes Agent (NousResearch) — same rationale, different\\nplumbing.\\n\\n## The invariant\\n\\nThe bridge preamble (tool notes + env block + memory block) is built\\n**once per ACP session** at session creation time. It is **never** rebuilt\\nwhile a session is live.\\n\\nConcretely:\\n\\n- `sessionManager.create()` calls `buildPreambleWithBreakdown(facts, userId)`\\n  exactly once and freezes the result into `session.memoryPreamble` +\\n  `session.preambleBreakdown`.\\n- `sessionManager.get()` returns the existing `ChatSession` object\\n  untouched when the session is still open (`!client.isClosed`). No\\n  re-read of the facts file, no re-measurement.\\n- `session.preambleInjected` is a latch: preamble text is included in\\n  the **first** `client.prompt()` call for that session and never again.\\n  Subsequent user turns send only the user text.\\n\\nThe preamble is only rebuilt when a **new session** is created, which\\nhappens in these four paths and no others:\\n\\n1. `get()` sees `existing.client.isClosed` → falls through to `create()`.\\n2. Session is idle past `config.sessionIdleMs` → `sweepIdle()` drops it\\n   and the next `get()` rebuilds.\\n3. Agent process crashes / exits unexpectedly → `exit` handler deletes\\n   the session from the map; next `get()` rebuilds.\\n4. User issues `/restart` or `/drop`, or the bridge shuts down and\\n   restarts.\\n\\n## Why\\n\\nThe Kiro / Codex / Claude ACP agents cache their system-prompt prefix\\naggressively at the LLM provider layer (Anthropic / OpenAI prompt\\ncaching, 5-minute TTL on most tiers). If the bridge were to rebuild\\npreamble mid-session and re-send it, **every user turn would invalidate\\nthe prefix cache**. For a 3–5 KB preamble that is re-hit 50 times in a\\nlong session, that's the difference between `~0 input-token cost for\\ncached content` and `full input-token cost × 50`. content.thinking:
The user is asking me to write a progress summary as a memory agent observing another Claude Code session. Let me analyze what happened:

The primary session had a minimal user request "[ASK:dream_entry2] ad", and Claude's full response discussed implementing dream suggestions across different ACP (Agent Control Protocol) adapters.

Key points from Claude's response:
1. The discussion is about how to surface "dream suggestions" (from some dream/analysis system) across different ACP implementations (Claude, Kiro, Codex)
2. Four options were compared (A: Preamble injection, B: /dreamtodo command, C: CLAUDE.md convention, D: Dream report ASK button)
3. The key insight: **preamble is injected by the bridge at session establishment, independent of ACP adapter**, so it works across all ACPs
4. Option C (CLAUDE.md) was ruled out because it's CLI-specific (Claude reads CLAUDE.md, Kiro needs steering, Codex needs AGENTS.md)
5. Option A emerged as the "zero-action automatic" solution
6. Option D can be layered on top for better UX
7. Implementation scope outlined: ~30 lines in sessionManager.ts, needs to check preamble-frozen-snapshot.md policies
8. User was presented with choices: do A, do A+D, or discuss more

The user selected "ad" which likely means "A + D按鈕" (A + D button option).

So the work being discussed is:
- Implementing automatic dream suggestion injection via preamble (Option A)
- Adding UI buttons in dream reports for easy access (Option D)
- This enables cross-ACP dream suggestion awareness without requiring manual user actions

Let me write this as an observation of what was discussed/decided in that session.

content.text:
<summary>
  <request>Evaluate cross-ACP dream suggestion surfacing approaches and select implementation strategy</request>
  <investigated>Four approaches for surfacing dream suggestions across different ACP adapters (Claude, Kiro, Codex) were compared: preamble injection, slash command, CLAUDE.md convention, and ASK button callbacks. The critical architectural insight examined was that preamble content is injected by the bridge layer at session establishment time, making it adapter-agnostic, while CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/steering files are CLI-specific loading mechanisms.</investigated>
  <learned>Preamble injection operates at the bridge prompt layer, independent of ACP adapter implementation. This makes it the only "zero-action automatic" approach that works universally. CLAUDE.md conventions only work in Claude ACP, requiring separate implementations in Kiro (steering) and Codex (AGENTS.md), creating a three-way maintenance burden. The bridge's working state block pattern (sessionManager.ts:225) provides a template for injecting pending dream suggestions automatically.</learned>
  <completed>Cross-ACP compatibility analysis completed. Option C (CLAUDE.md convention) ruled out due to adapter-specific limitations. Option A (preamble injection) selected as the core approach with Option D (ASK button) identified as a stackable UX enhancement. Implementation scope defined: ~30 lines in sessionManager.ts to read STATE.md, parse Last run timestamp, extract High Priority section, and inject a [Pending dream suggestions] block when Last run < 48h and suggestions exist.</completed>
  <next_steps>Implement Option A + D: add preamble injection logic to sessionManager.ts for automatic dream suggestion awareness, then add ASK buttons to dream reports for direct user interaction. Implementation must follow POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md checklist before modifying preamble assembly. Smoke test with npx tsc --noEmit and related check scripts.</next_steps>
  <notes>The user selected "ad" (A + D option), committing to both the automatic preamble injection and the optional UI button enhancement. The architectural insight about preamble vs CLI-specific config files was the key decision factor that eliminated the CLAUDE.md approach.</notes>
</summary>

MEMORY_DIR is passed through too so the mcp\\n    // server doesn't need to re-read .env.\\n    const env: Record<string, string> = { MEMORY_DIR: config.memoryDir };\\n    if (userId) env.TG_ACTIVE_USER_ID = String(userId);\\n\\n    const activeType = providerType ?? config.defaultProvider;\\n    const facts = userId ? await loadFacts(userId) : \\\"\\\";\\n\\n    // Try to compress recent facts via local LLM (graceful fallback if unavailable)\\n    let compressed: string | null = null;\\n    if (facts && activeType !== \\\"local\\\") {\\n      const lines = facts.split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/).filter(l => l.trim().length > 0);\\n      const tail = config.memoryPreambleTail;\\n      if (lines.length > tail) {\\n        const { compressFactsForPreamble: compress } = await import(\\\"./local-llm.js\\\");\\n        compressed = await compress(lines.slice(-tail));\\n      }\\n    }\\n    const breakdown = activeType === \\\"local\\\"\\n      ? buildLocalPreamble(facts, userId)\\n      : buildPreambleWithBreakdown(facts, userId, { compressed });\\n\\n    // Inject working-state from previous session if available (one-shot).\\n    const wsPath = path.join(config.memoryDir, `working-state-${chatId}.md`);\\n    let workingStateBlock = \\\"\\\";\\n    try {\\n      const ws = await readFile(wsPath, \\\"utf8\\\");\\n      workingStateBlock =\\n        \\\"\\\\n[Previous session working state — use as context to resume work]\\\\n\\\" +\\n        ws +\\n        \\\"\\\\nResume from `next_action` above. content.thinking:
The user is observing work on implementing dream suggestion surfacing in the telegram-kiro-bridge codebase. Let me analyze what's happening:

1. **Read POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md**: This is the policy document that governs how preamble is built and when it can be modified. The key insight is that preamble is built once per session at creation time and never rebuilt mid-session to preserve prompt caching benefits.

2. **Read src/sessionManager.ts (lines 195-274)**: This shows the `create()` method where preamble is assembled. I can see:
   - Facts loading and compression
   - Building preamble with breakdown
   - Injection of working-state block from previous session (lines 225-237)
   - Injection of session archive restoration block (lines 241-253)
   - Injection of relay task block (line 255)
   - Final preamble assembly: `breakdown.text + workingStateBlock + archiveBlock + relayTaskBlock`

3. **Read src/commands/dream.ts (lines 200-279)**: This shows the STATE.md generation logic. The dream routine:
   - Generates a report
   - Writes a three-tier Loop State to STATE.md with sections: High Priority, Watch List, Noise
   - Last run timestamp is included
   - Format is strictly defined with dash lists

Now I can understand what's being prepared: the developer is reading the relevant files to implement Option A (preamble injection of dream suggestions). The pattern is clear:
- Similar to `workingStateBlock` and `archiveBlock` injection
- Need to read STATE.md
- Parse Last run timestamp (check if < 48h)
- Extract High Priority section
- Inject as a new block in the preamble

This is discovery/investigation work, not yet implementation.

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Preamble injection architecture for dream suggestions</title>
  <subtitle>Identified existing preamble block patterns and frozen-snapshot policy constraints for adding dream suggestion awareness</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Preamble is built once per session in sessionManager.create() and never rebuilt mid-session to preserve LLM provider prompt cache efficiency</fact>
    <fact>sessionManager.ts line 225-237 shows working-state block injection pattern using one-shot file read and deletion</fact>
    <fact>sessionManager.ts line 255 assembles final preamble as breakdown.text + workingStateBlock + archiveBlock + relayTaskBlock</fact>
    <fact>dream.ts writes STATE.md with three-tier structure (High Priority, Watch List, Noise) and Last run timestamp in ISO format</fact>
    <fact>STATE.md format strictly enforces dash lists, 30-line limit, and "— 無" for empty sections</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer examined the preamble assembly architecture to prepare for implementing automatic dream suggestion surfacing. The preamble-frozen-snapshot.md policy establishes that preamble is built exactly once at session creation time and never rebuilt during a live session, enforcing this to preserve Anthropic/OpenAI prompt caching efficiency (5-minute TTL). The sessionManager.create() method shows three existing injection patterns: working-state blocks (one-shot file injection with deletion), session archive restoration blocks, and relay task blocks, all concatenated into the final preamble. The dream command writes STATE.md with a strict three-tier format including Last run timestamp, providing the source data for the planned injection. The architecture supports adding a new dream suggestion block following the same pattern: read STATE.md at session creation, parse Last run timestamp, extract High Priority section if within 48 hours, and inject as a new block between existing components.</narrative>

  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\index.ts\",\"offset\":185,\"limit\":110}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\telegram-kiro-bridge-main\\\\src\\\\index.ts\",\"content\":\"// Each handleX(ctx) contains the full body for one command, so it can be\\n// invoked both from bot.command() (slash command) and from the inline-button\\n// callback_query dispatcher below. A handler that depends on ctx.match (an\\n// argument) must gracefully handle the case where ctx.match is undefined —\\n// that's what happens when triggered from a button.\\n\\nbot.command(\\\"help\\\", handleHelp);\\nbot.command(\\\"menu\\\", handleMenu);\\nbot.command(\\\"hide\\\", handleHide);\\n\\n// Handle taps on the inline help keyboard.\\n//   help:<name>   → show that command's description (or return to menu)\\n//   run:<name>    → execute the command (dispatched through COMMAND_HANDLERS)\\nbot.on(\\\"callback_query:data\\\", async (ctx) => {\\n  if (!isAllowed(ctx)) {\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"未授權。\\\" });\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  const data = ctx.callbackQuery.data;\\n  const [kind, name] = data.split(\\\":\\\", 2);\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"help\\\") {\\n    if (name === \\\"_back\\\") {\\n      await ctx.editMessageText(HELP_INTRO, {\\n        parse_mode: \\\"HTML\\\",\\n        reply_markup: buildHelpMenuKeyboard(),\\n      });\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery();\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const spec = COMMAND_SPECS.find((s) => s.name === name);\\n    if (!spec) {\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"未知的指令。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    await ctx.editMessageText(renderCommandDetail(spec), {\\n      parse_mode: \\\"HTML\\\",\\n      reply_markup: buildCommandDetailKeyboard(spec),\\n    });\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery();\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"run\\\") {\\n    const spec = COMMAND_SPECS.find((s) => s.name === name);\\n    if (!spec) {\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"未知的指令。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (spec.takesArg) {\\n      // Commands that strictly require an argument (e.g. /sendfile <path>)\\n      // can't be run from a button; hint the user to type it.\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"需要參數，請直接輸入指令。\\\" });\\n      await ctx.reply(`請直接輸入 ${spec.usage} 來執行。`);\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const handler = COMMAND_HANDLERS[spec.name];\\n    if (!handler) {\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"此指令尚未支援按鈕執行。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: `執行 ${spec.usage} 中…` });\\n    try {\\n      await handler(ctx);\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      await ctx.reply(`執行失敗：${(err as Error).message}`);\\n    }\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"IL\\\") {\\n    await handleIntelCallback(ctx, data);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"SS\\\") {\\n    await handleSkillSearchCallback(ctx, data);\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"A\\\") {\\n    // ASK callback: data is `A:{question_id}:{key}`. split(\\\":\\\", 2) above\\n    // only gave us the first two fields, so re-split here to get the key.\\n    const parts = data.split(\\\":\\\");\\n    const questionId = parts[1] || \\\"\\\";\\n    const key = parts.slice(2).join(\\\":\\\"); content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Telegram callback query routing infrastructure</title>
  <subtitle>Identified existing callback_query dispatcher pattern for implementing dream suggestion ASK buttons</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>index.ts line 198 implements centralized callback_query dispatcher using bot.on("callback_query:data") with data pattern matching</fact>
    <fact>intel.ts provides callback patterns for candidate management operations (load, skip, write) with answerCallbackQuery feedback</fact>
    <fact>skillsearch.ts demonstrates callback-driven skill installation flow with ephemeral result storage</fact>
    <fact>Callback data uses colon-delimited format like "cmd:param" for routing to specific handlers</fact>
    <fact>All callback handlers call ctx.answerCallbackQuery() to acknowledge the button press and optionally show feedback text</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer examined the existing callback query infrastructure to understand how to implement ASK buttons for dream suggestions (Option D). The telegram-kiro-bridge uses a centralized dispatcher in index.ts that listens for callback_query:data events and routes based on string pattern matching. Two command modules already implement callback handlers: intel.ts for managing research candidates with operations like load/skip/write, and skillsearch.ts for interactive skill installation. The pattern is consistent: callback data uses colon-separated tokens (e.g., "intel:skip:123"), handlers perform the action, and ctx.answerCallbackQuery() acknowledges the button press with optional feedback text. This infrastructure can be reused for dream suggestions by adding a new callback pattern (e.g., "dream:action:item") that routes to a dream command handler, allowing the dream report to include inline buttons that trigger specific suggestion actions when pressed.</narrative>

    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>src/commands/skillsearch.ts</file>
    <file>src/index.ts</file>
  </files_read>

  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
This is discovering the ASK callback system which already exists in the codebase. The developer is examining the callback_query dispatcher to understand how to implement dream suggestion buttons (Option D). They found that:

1. The dispatcher handles different kinds of callbacks with prefixes: "help:", "run:", "IL:" (intel), "SS:" (skillsearch), and "A:" (ASK callbacks)
2. The ASK system (kind === "A") already provides a general-purpose inline button framework
3. ASK callbacks use format "A:{question_id}:{key}" with a registry system
4. When a button is pressed, the keyboard is stripped and the choice is handled in a follow-up message
5. There's an askRegistry that maps message IDs to question entries with options

This is key because the developer could either:
- Use the existing ASK system for dream suggestions (reuse infrastructure)
- Create a new callback kind for dream-specific buttons (e.g., "D:" for dream)

The ASK system seems like it could be reused since it's already designed for presenting choices to the user and routing the selection to handlers.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>ASK callback system for inline button interactions</title>
  <subtitle>Found existing general-purpose ASK framework that can be reused for dream suggestion buttons</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>index.ts line 267-295 implements ASK callback handler with format "A:{question_id}:{key}" and askRegistry for state management</fact>
    <fact>ASK callbacks strip the inline keyboard after selection using ctx.editMessageReplyMarkup to prevent duplicate taps</fact>
    <fact>askRegistry maps message_id to question entries containing options array with key and label fields</fact>
    <fact>ASK system preserves original message content when removing buttons, avoiding text loss from caption-only messages</fact>
    <fact>Callback dispatcher routes to specialized handlers: "IL" for intel, "SS" for skillsearch, "A" for ASK, and "run" for generic command execution</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer discovered the existing ASK callback system, a general-purpose inline button framework already implemented in the codebase. The system uses "A:{question_id}:{key}" callback data format and maintains an askRegistry that maps Telegram message IDs to question entries with multiple choice options. When a user taps a button, the handler strips the inline keyboard to prevent duplicate selections, preserves the original message content (important for caption-only messages), and routes the selected key to appropriate handlers. This infrastructure can be directly reused for dream suggestion buttons (Option D) without creating a new callback kind. The dream command can generate ASK-format buttons for each High Priority item, register them in askRegistry, and handle the selected action when users tap buttons like "處理建議" (handle suggestion) or specific suggestion items. This avoids duplicating the button infrastructure and integrates naturally with the existing callback routing pattern.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
    <file>src/index.ts</file>
  </files_read>

  </files_modified>
</observation>

項目…（或「— 無」）\\n *   ## Watch List (monitor) / ## Noise (ignored this run)\\n *\\n * 呼叫者：\\n *   • sessionManager.create() — preamble 注入 [Pending dream suggestions] block\\n *   • index.ts DT callback — dream 報告按鈕點擊時把 High Priority 丟給 agent\\n *\\n * 全部 fail-open：檔案缺失 / 格式不符 → 回 null，不拋錯。\\n */\\nimport { readFileSync } from \\\"node:fs\\\";\\nimport path from \\\"node:path\\\";\\nimport { config } from \\\"./config.js\\\";\\n\\nexport interface LoopStateHighPriority {\\n  lastRun: Date | null;\\n  /** dash-list 項目（已去掉「— 無」佔位）。可能為空陣列。 */\\n  items: string[];\\n}\\n\\nexport function loopStatePath(): string {\\n  return path.join(config.memoryDir, \\\"STATE.md\\\");\\n}\\n\\n/** 讀 STATE.md 並抽出 High Priority 項目。檔案不存在或無 High Priority\\n *  section 時回 null。 */\\nexport function readLoopStateHighPriority(): LoopStateHighPriority | null {\\n  let raw: string;\\n  try {\\n    raw = readFileSync(loopStatePath(), \\\"utf8\\\");\\n  } catch {\\n    return null;\\n  }\\n\\n  const lastRunMatch = raw.match(/^Last run:\\\\s*(.+)$/m);\\n  let lastRun: Date | null = null;\\n  if (lastRunMatch) {\\n    const d = new Date(lastRunMatch[1].trim());\\n    if (!Number.isNaN(d.getTime())) lastRun = d;\\n  }\\n\\n  // 取 `## High Priority` 到下一個 `## ` heading（或檔尾）之間的內容\\n  const sectionMatch = raw.match(/^## High Priority[^\\\\n]*\\\\n([\\\\s\\\\S]*?)(?=^## |$(?![\\\\s\\\\S]))/m);\\n  if (!sectionMatch) return null;\\n\\n  const items = sectionMatch[1]\\n    .split(/\\\\r?\\\\n/)\\n    .map((l) => l.trim())\\n    .filter((l) => l.startsWith(\\\"- \\\"))\\n    .map((l) => l.slice(2).trim())\\n    // dream 約定「某段沒東西就寫『— 無』」——不算實際項目\\n    .filter((l) => l.length > 0 && l !== \\\"— 無\\\" && l !== \\\"—\\\" && l !== \\\"無\\\");\\n\\n  return { lastRun, items };\\n}\\n\"}"</parameters>

content.thinking:
The developer just created a new module `loop-state.ts` that provides utilities for reading the STATE.md file produced by the /dream command. This is a key building block for implementing the preamble injection feature (Option A).

Key aspects:
1. It exports an interface `LoopStateHighPriority` with `lastRun: Date | null` and `items: string[]`
2. It provides `readLoopStateHighPriority()` function that:
   - Reads STATE.md from memory directory
   - Parses the "Last run:" timestamp
   - Extracts items from the "## High Priority" section
   - Filters out placeholder items like "— 無"
   - Returns null if file doesn't exist or has no High Priority section (fail-open)
3. Uses regex to extract the High Priority section between `## High Priority` and the next `##` heading
4. Fail-open design: no exceptions thrown, returns null on any error

This module will be used by:
- sessionManager.create() for preamble injection
- index.ts DT callback for dream report button handling

This is implementing Option A (preamble injection) as planned.

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Loop state parser for dream suggestion surfacing</title>
  <subtitle>Created parser module to extract High Priority items from STATE.md for preamble injection and callback handling</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>loop-state.ts exports readLoopStateHighPriority() function that parses STATE.md Last run timestamp and High Priority section items</fact>
    <fact>Parser uses regex /^## High Priority[^\n]*\n([\s\S]*?)(?=^## |$(?![\s\S]))/m to extract section content between headings</fact>
    <fact>Filter logic removes placeholder items "— 無", "—", "無" from dash-list results</fact>
    <fact>Fail-open design returns null on file missing or parse failure without throwing exceptions</fact>
    <fact>Module provides loopStatePath() helper returning path.join(config.memoryDir, "STATE.md")</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer implemented loop-state.ts, a dedicated parser module for reading the three-tier Loop State produced by /dream command. The module provides readLoopStateHighPriority() which reads STATE.md, extracts the ISO timestamp from "Last run:" line, and parses the High Priority section using regex to capture content between the section heading and the next ## marker or end-of-file. The parser filters dash-list items, removes dream.ts's placeholder "— 無" entries, and returns null on any failure (missing file, invalid format, no section) to ensure fail-open behavior. This module serves as the foundation for both Option A (preamble injection in sessionManager.create()) and Option D (ASK button callbacks in index.ts), providing a clean separation of STATE.md parsing logic from its two usage contexts. The implementation follows the existing bridge pattern of dedicated utility modules with clear interfaces and graceful degradation.</narrative>

   session._orphanFlushTimer = undefined;\\n    }\\n    const text = (session._orphanBuffer || \\\"\\\").trim();\\n    session._orphanBuffer = undefined;\\n    if (!text) return;\\n    if (!this.onOrphanTurn) {\\n      console.warn(\\n        `[orphan ${session.chatId}] buffered ${text.length} chars but no onOrphanTurn handler wired — dropping`,\\n      );\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    console.log(`[orphan ${session.chatId}] flushing orphan turn (${text.length} chars)`);\\n    logEvent(\\\"orphan_flush\\\", { chars: text.length }, { chatId: session.chatId, userId: session.userId });\\n    Promise.resolve(this.onOrphanTurn(session, text)).catch((err) =>\\n      console.error(`[orphan ${session.chatId}] onOrphanTurn failed:`, err),\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  resetTurn(session: ChatSession) {\\n    // Flush any pending orphan output before a new live turn clears the\\n    // buffers, so background continuation isn't lost or interleaved with the\\n    // upcoming turn's streaming placeholder.\\n    if (session._orphanBuffer || session._orphanFlushTimer) this.flushOrphan(session);\\n    session._orphanSuppressUntil = undefined;\\n    session.buffer = \\\"\\\";\\n    session.thinkingBuffer = undefined;\\n    session.toolLog = [];\\n    session.stderrTail = \\\"\\\";\\n    session._toolInFlight = false;\\n    session._streamingDelegates = undefined;\\n    session._toolTitles?.clear();\\n    // push turn 邊界 marker 但「不清空」_recentToolNames——硬清空會讓每 turn 第一個\\n    // tool 的 precedingTools 永遠空；marker 讓下游 analyzer 自己切斷跨 turn combo。\\n    if (session._recentToolNames?.length) {\\n      session._recentToolNames = appendToolName(session._recentToolNames, TURN_MARKER);\\n    }\\n    this.startInactivityTimer(session);\\n  }\\n\\n Genuine later background output re-arms fresh.\\n    if (s._orphanFlushTimer) { clearTimeout(s._orphanFlushTimer); s._orphanFlushTimer = undefined; }\\n    s._orphanBuffer = undefined;\\n    // Suppress capture across the force-kill window so trailing chunks don't leak.\\n    s._orphanSuppressUntil = Date.now() + 20_000;\\n\\n    // Force-kill escalation: if CLI doesn't exit the turn within 15s,\\n    // kill the process tree and re-create on next message.\\n    if (!s.client.isClosed) {\\n      const timer = setTimeout(async () => {\\n        if (s.client.isClosed) return;\\n        console.warn(`[cancel] chat ${chatId} CLI still alive after 15s — force-killing`);\\n        await s.client.close();\\n      }, 15_000);\\n      timer.unref?.();\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  /** Enable drop-all mode: silently discard messages sent before now. */\\n  enableDropAll(chatId: number) { this._dropBefore.set(chatId, Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)); }\\n  /** Disable drop-all mode. */\\n  disableDropAll(chatId: number) { this._dropBefore.delete(chatId); }\\n  /** Check if a message should be dropped (sent before the cutoff). */\\n  shouldDrop(chatId: number, messageDate: number): boolean {\\n    const cutoff = this._dropBefore.get(chatId);\\n    if (!cutoff) return false;\\n    if (messageDate <= cutoff) return true;\\n    // Message is newer than cutoff — auto-clear and let it through\\n    this._dropBefore.delete(chatId);\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n\\n  peek(chatId: number): ChatSession | undefined {\\n    return this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n  }\\n\\n  size(): number {\\n    return this.sessions.size;\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Persist a closing session's archive, or carry the prior archive forward.\\n   *\\n   * A \\\"substantive\\\" close (>= config.archiveMinTurns turns with some assistant\\n   * output) is exported — including a close-time LLM summary — and saved. n + h.text.length : n),\\n      0,\\n    );\\n    const substantive = s.history.length >= config.archiveMinTurns && assistantChars > 0;\\n    try {\\n      if (substantive) {\\n        await saveArchive(await exportSession(s, closeReason));\\n      } else if (s.priorArchive) {\\n        // Trivial close — keep the prior, more-substantive archive.\\n        await saveArchive(s.priorArchive);\\n        console.log(`[archive] chat ${s.chatId}: trivial close (${s.history.length} turns), carried prior archive forward`);\\n      } else if (s.history.length > 0) {\\n        // No prior to fall back to — persist what little we have.\\n        await saveArchive(await exportSession(s, closeReason));\\n      }\\n    } catch (e) {\\n      console.error(`[session ${s.chatId}] archive failed:`, e);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  async drop(chatId: number, closeReason: \\\"manual\\\" | \\\"reset\\\" | \\\"restart\\\" = \\\"manual\\\") {\\n    const s = this.sessions.get(chatId);\\n    if (!s) return;\\n    this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n    // Archive session state before closing (summary + trivial carry-forward)\\n    if (s.history.length > 0 || s.priorArchive) {\\n      await this.archiveOnClose(s, closeReason);\\n    }\\n    if (this.onBeforeClose) {\\n      try { await this.onBeforeClose(s); } catch (e) {\\n        console.error(`[session ${chatId}] onBeforeClose failed:`, e);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    await s.client.close();\\n  }\\n\\n  private async sweepIdle() {\\n    const now = Date.now();\\n    for (const [chatId, s] of this.sessions) {\\n      // --- Runtime Health Monitor ---\\n      // Detect subprocess that silently died (pid no longer valid) but\\n      // exit event hasn't fired yet (possible on Windows when parent shell\\n      // exits but Node doesn't see the signal).\\n      const clientPid = \\\"pid\\\" in s.client ? // skip idle check for this session\\n        }\\n      }\\n\\n      // --- Idle timeout sweep ---\\n      if (now - s.lastUsed > config.sessionIdleMs) {\\n        console.log(`[session] dropping idle chat ${chatId}`);\\n        this.sessions.delete(chatId);\\n        // Archive session state before closing (summary + trivial carry-forward)\\n        if (s.history.length > 0 || s.priorArchive) {\\n          await this.archiveOnClose(s, \\\"idle\\\");\\n        }\\n        if (this.onBeforeClose) {\\n          try { await this.onBeforeClose(s); 3. Checks freshness: Last run < 48h
4. Constructs dreamStateBlock with High Priority items formatted as dash list

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Automatic dream suggestion preamble injection</title>
  <subtitle>Implemented Option A preamble injection to surface High Priority dream items automatically at session creation across all ACP adapters</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>sessionManager.ts create() now reads STATE.md via readLoopStateHighPriority() and injects a dreamStateBlock when Last run is within 48 hours</fact>
    <fact>dreamStateBlock format is "[Pending dream suggestions]" block with High Priority items as dash list, instructions mapping "處理建議" to these items, and STATE.md file path reference</fact>
    <fact>Injection occurs once at session creation in the preamble assembly chain: breakdown.text + workingStateBlock + archiveBlock + dreamStateBlock + relayTaskBlock</fact>
    <fact>48-hour freshness gate prevents injecting stale suggestions from old dream runs</fact>
    <fact>Implementation follows frozen-snapshot policy: dreamStateBlock is built once in create() and frozen with preamble, never rebuilt mid-session</fact>
    <fact>Fail-open design uses try-catch to ensure STATE.md missing or parse failure does not break session creation</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer implemented Option A, the automatic preamble injection approach for surfacing dream suggestions. When sessionManager.create() builds a new session, it now reads STATE.md via the loop-state.ts parser, checks if the Last run timestamp is within 48 hours (freshness gate), and if High Priority items exist, constructs a dreamStateBlock formatted as a bracketed section with dash-list items. The block includes explicit instructions that phrases like "處理建議" or "dream 建議" refer to these items, and points to STATE.md for the full three-tier list. This block is inserted into the preamble assembly chain between archiveBlock and relayTaskBlock, making it part of the frozen preamble that all ACP adapters (Claude, Kiro, Codex) receive at session establishment. The implementation strictly follows the frozen-snapshot policy documented in POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md: the block is built exactly once in create() and never rebuilt mid-session, preserving LLM prompt cache efficiency. The fail-open design ensures that STATE.md missing or parse errors are caught and logged but do not disrupt session creation. This is the first half of the "ad" implementation (A + D buttons), providing zero-action automatic awareness across all adapters.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/sessionManager.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

    <fact>dream.ts depends on deps() module providing sessions, runPrompt, and commandHandlers accessors for telegram bot context</fact>

    <fact>Dream module imports from multiple subsystems: dream-config, hit-log, facts-store, page-index, event-log, activity-log, shared-sync</fact>

  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer examined the dream command module structure to understand integration points for adding ASK buttons (Option D). The module uses a deps() accessor pattern to obtain sessions, runPrompt, and commandHandlers without circular dependencies. The triggerSharedReview() helper demonstrates the existing pattern for programmatically invoking agent prompts within command handlers: get the session via sessions.get(), construct a prompt string, and call runPrompt() with the session and prompt. This same pattern can be used to implement the ASK button callback handler: when a user taps a dream suggestion button, the callback handler would retrieve the corresponding High Priority item from STATE.md, construct a prompt asking the agent to handle that item, and invoke runPrompt() to process it. The Context type from grammy is the standard telegram bot context passed to all handlers, including callback_query handlers. The module's access to MEMORY_DIR confirms it operates in the same memory directory tree where STATE.md is stored, ensuring path consistency.</narrative>

content.thinking: The developer added an import for the loop-state.ts module functions into dream.ts. This is setting up to add the ASK button functionality (Option D) by importing the necessary parser functions to read High Priority items from STATE.md.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>change</type>
  <title>Import loop state parser into dream command module</title>
  <subtitle>Added readLoopStateHighPriority and loopStatePath imports to enable ASK button callback implementation</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>dream.ts now imports readLoopStateHighPriority and loopStatePath from loop-state.js module</fact>
    <fact>Import positioned after shared-sync import and before MEMORY_DIR constant declaration</fact>
    <fact>These functions will enable dream command to read STATE.md High Priority items for callback button functionality</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer added imports for the loop state parser functions to the dream command module. By importing readLoopStateHighPriority and loopStatePath, the dream.ts module gains the ability to read and parse High Priority items from STATE.md. This is the setup step for implementing Option D (ASK buttons), where the dream report will include inline buttons that let users tap to handle specific suggestions. The callback handler will use these imported functions to retrieve the corresponding High Priority item when a button is pressed, then construct and execute an agent prompt to process that item. This completes the dependency setup needed to add button generation to the dream report and implement the callback handler.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/commands/dream.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
The developer has added code to send an inline button notification when High Priority items are written to STATE.md during the dream routine. This is part of implementing Option D (ASK buttons). Let me break down what was added:

1. After writing STATE.md, it reads back the High Priority section using readLoopStateHighPriority()
2. If there are High Priority items, it sends a notification message with an inline button
3. The button text is "▶️ 處理 High Priority" (Process High Priority)
4. The callback_data is "DT:hp" - this is the callback pattern for dream tasks
5. The button send is wrapped in .catch() to ensure failures don't break the dream routine

This creates the UI button that users can tap to handle suggestions. The callback_data "DT:hp" will be routed to a callback handler that needs to be implemented next in index.ts to complete Option D.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Dream suggestion notification button implementation</title>
  <subtitle>Added inline button to dream report that sends notification when High Priority items exist in STATE.md</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>handleDream() now reads STATE.md after writing via readLoopStateHighPriority() to check for High Priority items</fact>
    <fact>When High Priority items exist, sends notification message with inline keyboard button labeled "▶️ 處理 High Priority"</fact>
    <fact>Callback data format is "DT:hp" following existing callback pattern for dream tasks</fact>
    <fact>Button send wrapped in .catch() to ensure failures don't interrupt dream routine completion</fact>
    <fact>Notification displays count of High Priority items: "Loop State 已更新：High Priority N 項待處理"</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer implemented the first part of Option D (ASK buttons) by adding a notification with an inline button when the dream routine writes High Priority items to STATE.md. After the agent generates and saves the three-tier Loop State, the code re-reads STATE.md using readLoopStateHighPriority() to check if High Priority items exist. When items are present, it sends a Telegram message showing the count and includes an inline keyboard button labeled "▶️ 處理 High Priority" (Process High Priority) with callback_data "DT:hp". This follows the existing callback routing pattern where "DT" indicates a dream task callback and "hp" specifies the High Priority action. The button send is wrapped in a catch handler to ensure any Telegram API failures don't prevent the dream routine from completing successfully. This creates the user-facing entry point for handling suggestions; the next step will be implementing the "DT:hp" callback handler in index.ts to respond when users tap this button by reading the current High Priority items and invoking the agent to process them.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>src/commands/dream.ts</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

content.thinking:
The developer just implemented the callback handler for the dream suggestion button. This completes the Option D implementation. Let me analyze what was added:

1. A new exported function `handleDreamTodoCallback` that handles the "DT:hp" callback
2. It:
   - Checks authorization
   - Reads STATE.md using readLoopStateHighPriority()
   - If no High Priority items exist, shows a message that they may have been handled
   - Gets the chat ID from context
   - Removes the inline keyboard button to prevent duplicate taps
   - Acknowledges the callback with a count message
   - Gets the session and constructs a prompt asking the agent to process the High Priority items
   - Tells the agent to use <<ASK:...>> for decisions and to update STATE.md as items are completed
   - Calls runPrompt to start the agent processing

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Dream High Priority callback handler implementation</title>
  <subtitle>Implemented handleDreamTodoCallback to process button taps by reading current STATE.md and invoking agent with High Priority items</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>handleDreamTodoCallback() exported from dream.ts handles "DT:hp" callback_data when users tap the High Priority button</fact>
    <fact>Handler re-reads STATE.md at callback time via readLoopStateHighPriority() ensuring always-current data even if STATE.md was updated since button creation</fact>
    <fact>Removes inline keyboard using ctx.editMessageReplyMarkup to prevent duplicate button taps on same message</fact>
    <fact>Constructs agent prompt listing all current High Priority items with instructions to process each, use ASK for decisions, and update STATE.md upon completion</fact>
    <fact>Graceful degradation with answerCallbackQuery showing "目前無 High Priority 待辦（可能已處理完）" when items were already handled</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer implemented the callback handler that completes Option D (ASK buttons). The handleDreamTodoCallback function responds when users tap the "▶️ 處理 High Priority" button created earlier. The handler follows the existing callback pattern: checks authorization, acknowledges the tap with answerCallbackQuery, removes the button to prevent duplicate executions, and processes the request. Critically, it re-reads STATE.md at callback time rather than using stale button state, ensuring the agent always receives the current High Priority list even if items were added/removed since the button was created. The prompt instructs the agent to process items sequentially, use ASK for user decisions, and update STATE.md to mark completion. This design supports the "永遠最新" (always current) requirement noted in the button implementation comment. The next step is wiring this handler into the callback dispatcher in index.ts to route "DT:hp" callbacks to this function, completing the full A+D implementation.</narrative>

    <concept>what-changed</concept>
  </concepts>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Dream command exports imported in index.ts</title>
  <subtitle>Located existing dream.ts import statement to add handleDreamTodoCallback for callback routing</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>index.ts line 59 imports handleDream, handleDailyLog, handleArtifactCleanup, handleUtilityReport, handleSharedPull, handleSharedSync, handleDocUpdate from ./commands/dream.js</fact>
    <fact>Import statement uses destructured named imports from dream.js module</fact>
    <fact>handleDreamTodoCallback needs to be added to this import list to make it available for callback dispatcher routing</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer located the existing import statement in index.ts that brings in dream command handlers. This import statement on line 59 uses destructured named imports to pull in seven handler functions from the dream.js module. To complete the callback routing implementation, handleDreamTodoCallback needs to be added to this import list so the callback dispatcher can invoke it when users tap the "DT:hp" button. After updating the import, the next step will be adding a routing case in the callback_query dispatcher to detect "DT:hp" callback data and call handleDreamTodoCallback, similar to how "IL" routes to handleIntelCallback and "SS" routes to handleSkillSearchCallback.</narrative>

  </concepts>

    <file>src/index.ts</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

Two ops today:\\n    //   - `sched:rm:<id>` — delete a single schedule, re-render the list.\\n    //   - `sched:cancel`  — no-op escape hatch; just strips the inline\\n    //                       keyboard from the current message so a user\\n    //                       can dismiss all the 🗑 buttons without risking\\n    //                       a fat-finger tap.\\n    // Future subcommands (e.g. \\\"run\\\") can share this branch.\\n    //\\n    // Policy:\\n    //   - Silent toast on success/failure via answerCallbackQuery.\\n    //   - For `rm`, re-render the whole list by editing the original\\n    //     message so the removed row disappears; if the list is now\\n    //     empty, strip the keyboard and change the text to \\\"目前沒有排程。\\\".\\n    //   - For `cancel`, only edit reply_markup (keep the text) so the\\n    //     user still sees what schedules exist — they just can't tap any.\\n    //   - Permission: bridge is 1-on-1 today, but be defensive — reject\\n    //     `rm` when entry.userId !== ctx.from?.id. `cancel` skips the\\n    //     check because it's non-destructive.\\n    const parts = data.split(\\\":\\\");\\n    const op = parts[1];\\n    const id = parts.slice(2).join(\\\":\\\"); // tolerate ':' in id (shouldn't happen; uuid slice)\\n    if (op === \\\"cancel\\\") {\\n      // Dismiss-keyboard escape hatch. No permission check: the button only\\n      // strips reply_markup from the message the user is already looking at,\\n      // so at worst a different user in a (hypothetical) group chat hides\\n      // the keyboard from themselves. No schedule state changes.\\n      try {\\n        await ctx.editMessageReplyMarkup({ reply_markup: undefined });\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        // \\\"message is not modified\\\" is fine — keyboard was already gone.\\n        console.warn(\\n          `[sched] cancel clear-keyboard failed:`,\\n          (err as Error).message,\\n        );\\n      }\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"已取消,未刪除任何排程。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (op !== \\\"rm\\\" || !id) {\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"未知的操作。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const entry = scheduleStore.get(id);\\n    if (!entry) {\\n      // Already gone — stale button (user double-tapped, or fired since the\\n      // list was rendered). Tell them, then try to redraw the list so the\\n      // phantom row disappears.\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"排程已不存在。\\\" });\\n      const chatId = ctx.chat?.id;\\n      if (chatId !== undefined) {\\n        const { text, keyboard } = renderScheduleList(chatId);\\n        try {\\n          await ctx.editMessageText(text, {\\n            reply_markup: keyboard ?? { inline_keyboard: [] },\\n          });\\n        } catch (err) {\\n          // editMessageText throws \\\"message is not modified\\\" if nothing\\n          // changed — that's fine, nothing to do.\\n          console.warn(\\n            `[sched] redraw after stale rm failed:`,\\n            (err as Error).message,\\n          );\\n        }\\n      }\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (ctx.from?.id !== undefined && entry.userId !== ctx.from.id) {\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"這不是你的排程。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const chatId = ctx.chat?.id;\\n    if (chatId !== undefined && entry.chatId !== chatId) {\\n      // Button from another chat somehow reached here — refuse.\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"排程不屬於這個聊天室。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    scheduler.disarm(id);\\n    await scheduleStore.remove(id);\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: `已移除 ${id}。` });\\n\\n    // Redraw the list in place so the user sees the new state without\\n    // scrolling back to re-run /schedule list.\\n    if (chatId !== undefined) {\\n      const { text, keyboard } = renderScheduleList(chatId);\\n      try {\\n        await ctx.editMessageText(text, {\\n          reply_markup: keyboard ?? { inline_keyboard: [] },\\n        });\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        console.warn(\\n          `[sched] redraw after rm failed:`,\\n          (err as Error).message,\\n        );\\n      }\\n    }\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"SP\\\") {\\n    // SKILL_PROPOSE callback: data is `SP:{name}:{yes|no}`.\\n    const parts = data.split(\\\":\\\");\\n    const skillName = parts[1] || \\\"\\\";\\n    const choice = parts[2] || \\\"\\\";\\n    const originalText = ctx.callbackQuery.message?.text ?? \\\"\\\";\\n    if (choice === \\\"no\\\") {\\n      try {\\n        await ctx.editMessageText(`${originalText}\\\\n\\\\n→ 已略過`);\\n      } catch { /* ignore */ }\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"已略過\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (choice === \\\"yes\\\") {\\n      try {\\n        await ctx.editMessageText(`${originalText}\\\\n\\\\n→ 開始萃取 skill…`);\\n      } catch { /* ignore */ }\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"開始萃取…\\\" });\\n      const chatId = ctx.chat?.id;\\n      if (chatId === undefined) return;\\n      let session: ChatSession;\\n      try {\\n        session = await sessions.get(chatId, ctx.from?.id);\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        await ctx.reply(`Failed to start agent: ${(err as Error).message}`);\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      const skillDir = detectSkillDir();\\n      const prompt =\\n        `[系統] 使用者確認要把「${skillName}」寫成 skill。\\\\n` +\\n        `請依照 memory-to-skill 和 writing-skills 的規範，在 ${skillDir}/ms-${skillName}/ 建立 SKILL.md。\\\\n\\\\n` +\\n        `規則：\\\\n` +\\n        `- 先讀 ${skillDir}/writing-skills/SKILL.md 確認格式規範\\\\n` +\\n        `- 檢查 ${skillDir}/ 是否已有同名或高度重疊的 skill，有就 append/rewrite\\\\n` +\\n        `- YAML frontmatter 的 description 用「Use when…」開頭\\\\n` +\\n        `- 內容來自本次對話中的經驗/模式\\\\n` +\\n        `- 完成後 remember() 記錄新 skill 已建立`;\\n      await runPrompt(ctx, session, prompt, { kind: \\\"meta\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery();\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  await ctx.answerCallbackQuery();\\n});\\n\\nbot.command(\\\"start\\\", (ctx) =>\\n  ctx.reply(\\n    \\\"Hi, I'm bridged to Kiro. Must happen after COMMAND_HANDLERS is declared; scheduler.armAll()\\n// runs later (after bot.start), so handlers are always ready by firing time.\\nscheduler.setCommandHandlers(COMMAND_HANDLERS);\\n\\n// Inject shared deps into session-extract module.\\ninitSessionExtract({\\n  bot,\\n  sessions,\\n  MEMORY_DIR,\\n  RESTART_PENDING_FILE,\\n  PENDING_EXTRACT_FILE,\\n  getRestartPendingChatId: () => restartPendingChatId,\\n  setRestartPendingChatId: (v) => { restartPendingChatId = v; },\\n});\\n\\n// Inject shared deps into working-state module.\\ninitWorkingState({ sessions, MEMORY_DIR });\\n\\n// Inject shared deps into run-prompt module.\\ninitRunPrompt({\\n  bot,\\n  sessions,\\n  rateLimitedUntil,\\n  onRateLimitRecovery,\\n  queuePendingMessage,\\n  handleRestart,\\n  STREAM_EDIT_INTERVAL_MS,\\n  MEMORY_DIR,\\n  scheduler,\\n  scheduleStore,\\n});\\n\\n// Inject shared deps into command modules (avoids circular imports).\\ninitCommandDeps({\\n  isAllowed,\\n  sessions,\\n  runPrompt,\\n  commandHandlers: COMMAND_HANDLERS,\\n  cancelPendingContinuation,\\n  formatRelative,\\n  scheduleStore,\\n  scheduler,\\n  extractFromSession,\\n  parseFactsJson,\\n  saveTranscript,\\n  estimateTokens,\\n  buildHelpMenuKeyboard,\\n  buildReplyKeyboard,\\n  helpIntro: HELP_INTRO,\\n  sendMessage: async (chatId, text) => {\\n    await bot.api.sendMessage(chatId, text);\\n  },\\n});\\nstartIntelPodcastWorker();\\n\\nbot.command(\\\"whoami\\\", handleWhoami);\\nbot.command(\\\"reset\\\", handleReset);\\nbot.command(\\\"cancel\\\", handleCancel);\\nbot.command(\\\"restart\\\", (ctx) => handleRestart(ctx));\\nbot.command(\\\"save\\\", handleSave);\\nbot.command(\\\"handoff\\\", handleHandoff);\\nbot.hears(/^\\\\/收工(?:@\\\\w+)?(?:\\\\s|$)/i, handleHandoff);\\nbot.command(\\\"sendfile\\\", handleSendfile);\\nbot.command(\\\"context\\\", handleContext);\\nbot.command(\\\"usage\\\", handleUsage);\\nbot.command(\\\"remember\\\", handleRemember);\\nbot.command(\\\"memory\\\", handleMemory);\\nbot.command(\\\"summary\\\", handleSummary);\\n// /MemoryToSkill — Telegram's setMyCommands requires lowercase, but users may\\n// also type the CamelCase form the help menu displays. 0) + 1;\\n      groupTurnCounters.set(key, turns);\\n      if (turns > GROUP_MAX_BOT_TURNS) {\\n        groupTurnCounters.set(key, 0);\\n        await ctx.reply(\\\"⚠️ 已達群組 bot 連續回覆上限，暫停回應。\\\");\\n        return;\\n      }\\n    } else {\\n      // 人類發話時重置計數器\\n      const key = `group_turns_${chatId}`;\\n      groupTurnCounters.set(key, 0);\\n      getRelayLoopGuard()?.reset();\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // AB-7b: a real user message always preempts a pending goal\\n  // continuation.     <fact>Import statement updated from 7 to 8 exported handlers from dream.js module</fact>

The import list was extended from seven handlers (handleDream, handleDailyLog, handleArtifactCleanup, handleUtilityReport, handleSharedPull, handleSharedSync, handleDocUpdate) to eight by appending handleDreamTodoCallback at the end. This makes the callback handler function available in index.ts scope, preparing it for integration into the callback_query dispatcher. Two ops today:\\n    //   - `sched:rm:<id>` — delete a single schedule, re-render the list.\\n    //   - `sched:cancel`  — no-op escape hatch; just strips the inline\\n    //                       keyboard from the current message so a user\\n    //                       can dismiss all the 🗑 buttons without risking\\n    //                       a fat-finger tap.\\n    // Future subcommands (e.g. \\\"run\\\") can share this branch.\\n    //\\n    // Policy:\\n    //   - Silent toast on success/failure via answerCallbackQuery.\\n    //   - For `rm`, re-render the whole list by editing the original\\n    //     message so the removed row disappears; if the list is now\\n    //     empty, strip the keyboard and change the text to \\\"目前沒有排程。\\\".\\n    //   - For `cancel`, only edit reply_markup (keep the text) so the\\n    //     user still sees what schedules exist — they just can't tap any.\\n    //   - Permission: bridge is 1-on-1 today, but be defensive — reject\\n    //     `rm` when entry.userId !== ctx.from?.id. `cancel` skips the\\n    //     check because it's non-destructive.\\n    const parts = data.split(\\\":\\\");\\n    const op = parts[1];\\n    const id = parts.slice(2).join(\\\":\\\"); // tolerate ':' in id (shouldn't happen; uuid slice)\\n    if (op === \\\"cancel\\\") {\\n      // Dismiss-keyboard escape hatch. No permission check: the button only\\n      // strips reply_markup from the message the user is already looking at,\\n      // so at worst a different user in a (hypothetical) group chat hides\\n      // the keyboard from themselves. No schedule state changes.\\n      try {\\n        await ctx.editMessageReplyMarkup({ reply_markup: undefined });\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        // \\\"message is not modified\\\" is fine — keyboard was already gone.\\n        console.warn(\\n          `[sched] cancel clear-keyboard failed:`,\\n          (err as Error).message,\\n        );\\n      }\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"已取消,未刪除任何排程。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (op !== \\\"rm\\\" || !id) {\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"未知的操作。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const entry = scheduleStore.get(id);\\n    if (!entry) {\\n      // Already gone — stale button (user double-tapped, or fired since the\\n      // list was rendered). Tell them, then try to redraw the list so the\\n      // phantom row disappears.\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"排程已不存在。\\\" });\\n      const chatId = ctx.chat?.id;\\n      if (chatId !== undefined) {\\n        const { text, keyboard } = renderScheduleList(chatId);\\n        try {\\n          await ctx.editMessageText(text, {\\n            reply_markup: keyboard ?? { inline_keyboard: [] },\\n          });\\n        } catch (err) {\\n          // editMessageText throws \\\"message is not modified\\\" if nothing\\n          // changed — that's fine, nothing to do.\\n          console.warn(\\n            `[sched] redraw after stale rm failed:`,\\n            (err as Error).message,\\n          );\\n        }\\n      }\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (ctx.from?.id !== undefined && entry.userId !== ctx.from.id) {\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"這不是你的排程。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    const chatId = ctx.chat?.id;\\n    if (chatId !== undefined && entry.chatId !== chatId) {\\n      // Button from another chat somehow reached here — refuse.\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"排程不屬於這個聊天室。\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    scheduler.disarm(id);\\n    await scheduleStore.remove(id);\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: `已移除 ${id}。` });\\n\\n    // Redraw the list in place so the user sees the new state without\\n    // scrolling back to re-run /schedule list.\\n    if (chatId !== undefined) {\\n      const { text, keyboard } = renderScheduleList(chatId);\\n      try {\\n        await ctx.editMessageText(text, {\\n          reply_markup: keyboard ?? { inline_keyboard: [] },\\n        });\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        console.warn(\\n          `[sched] redraw after rm failed:`,\\n          (err as Error).message,\\n        );\\n      }\\n    }\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (kind === \\\"SP\\\") {\\n    // SKILL_PROPOSE callback: data is `SP:{name}:{yes|no}`.\\n    const parts = data.split(\\\":\\\");\\n    const skillName = parts[1] || \\\"\\\";\\n    const choice = parts[2] || \\\"\\\";\\n    const originalText = ctx.callbackQuery.message?.text ?? \\\"\\\";\\n    if (choice === \\\"no\\\") {\\n      try {\\n        await ctx.editMessageText(`${originalText}\\\\n\\\\n→ 已略過`);\\n      } catch { /* ignore */ }\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"已略過\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    if (choice === \\\"yes\\\") {\\n      try {\\n        await ctx.editMessageText(`${originalText}\\\\n\\\\n→ 開始萃取 skill…`);\\n      } catch { /* ignore */ }\\n      await ctx.answerCallbackQuery({ text: \\\"開始萃取…\\\" });\\n      const chatId = ctx.chat?.id;\\n      if (chatId === undefined) return;\\n      let session: ChatSession;\\n      try {\\n        session = await sessions.get(chatId, ctx.from?.id);\\n      } catch (err) {\\n        await ctx.reply(`Failed to start agent: ${(err as Error).message}`);\\n        return;\\n      }\\n      const skillDir = detectSkillDir();\\n      const prompt =\\n        `[系統] 使用者確認要把「${skillName}」寫成 skill。\\\\n` +\\n        `請依照 memory-to-skill 和 writing-skills 的規範，在 ${skillDir}/ms-${skillName}/ 建立 SKILL.md。\\\\n\\\\n` +\\n        `規則：\\\\n` +\\n        `- 先讀 ${skillDir}/writing-skills/SKILL.md 確認格式規範\\\\n` +\\n        `- 檢查 ${skillDir}/ 是否已有同名或高度重疊的 skill，有就 append/rewrite\\\\n` +\\n        `- YAML frontmatter 的 description 用「Use when…」開頭\\\\n` +\\n        `- 內容來自本次對話中的經驗/模式\\\\n` +\\n        `- 完成後 remember() 記錄新 skill 已建立`;\\n      await runPrompt(ctx, session, prompt, { kind: \\\"meta\\\" });\\n      return;\\n    }\\n    await ctx.answerCallbackQuery();\\n    return;\\n  }\\n\\n  await ctx.answerCallbackQuery();\\n});\\n\\nbot.command(\\\"start\\\", (ctx) =>\\n  ctx.reply(\\n    \\\"Hi, I'm bridged to Kiro. Must happen after COMMAND_HANDLERS is declared; scheduler.armAll()\\n// runs later (after bot.start), so handlers are always ready by firing time.\\nscheduler.setCommandHandlers(COMMAND_HANDLERS);\\n\\n// Inject shared deps into session-extract module.\\ninitSessionExtract({\\n  bot,\\n  sessions,\\n  MEMORY_DIR,\\n  RESTART_PENDING_FILE,\\n  PENDING_EXTRACT_FILE,\\n  getRestartPendingChatId: () => restartPendingChatId,\\n  setRestartPendingChatId: (v) => { restartPendingChatId = v; },\\n});\\n\\n// Inject shared deps into working-state module.\\ninitWorkingState({ sessions, MEMORY_DIR });\\n\\n// Inject shared deps into run-prompt module.\\ninitRunPrompt({\\n  bot,\\n  sessions,\\n  rateLimitedUntil,\\n  onRateLimitRecovery,\\n  queuePendingMessage,\\n  handleRestart,\\n  STREAM_EDIT_INTERVAL_MS,\\n  MEMORY_DIR,\\n  scheduler,\\n  scheduleStore,\\n});\\n\\n// Inject shared deps into command modules (avoids circular imports).\\ninitCommandDeps({\\n  isAllowed,\\n  sessions,\\n  runPrompt,\\n  commandHandlers: COMMAND_HANDLERS,\\n  cancelPendingContinuation,\\n  formatRelative,\\n  scheduleStore,\\n  scheduler,\\n  extractFromSession,\\n  parseFactsJson,\\n  saveTranscript,\\n  estimateTokens,\\n  buildHelpMenuKeyboard,\\n  buildReplyKeyboard,\\n  helpIntro: HELP_INTRO,\\n  sendMessage: async (chatId, text) => {\\n    await bot.api.sendMessage(chatId, text);\\n  },\\n});\\nstartIntelPodcastWorker();\\n\\nbot.command(\\\"whoami\\\", handleWhoami);\\nbot.command(\\\"reset\\\", handleReset);\\nbot.command(\\\"cancel\\\", handleCancel);\\nbot.command(\\\"restart\\\", (ctx) => handleRestart(ctx));\\nbot.command(\\\"save\\\", handleSave);\\nbot.command(\\\"handoff\\\", handleHandoff);\\nbot.hears(/^\\\\/收工(?:@\\\\w+)?(?:\\\\s|$)/i, handleHandoff);\\nbot.command(\\\"sendfile\\\", handleSendfile);\\nbot.command(\\\"context\\\", handleContext);\\nbot.command(\\\"usage\\\", handleUsage);\\nbot.command(\\\"remember\\\", handleRemember);\\nbot.command(\\\"memory\\\", handleMemory);\\nbot.command(\\\"summary\\\", handleSummary);\\n// /MemoryToSkill — Telegram's setMyCommands requires lowercase, but users may\\n// also type the CamelCase form the help menu displays. 0) + 1;\\n      groupTurnCounters.set(key, turns);\\n      if (turns > GROUP_MAX_BOT_TURNS) {\\n        groupTurnCounters.set(key, 0);\\n        await ctx.reply(\\\"⚠️ 已達群組 bot 連續回覆上限，暫停回應。\\\");\\n        return;\\n      }\\n    } else {\\n      // 人類發話時重置計數器\\n      const key = `group_turns_${chatId}`;\\n      groupTurnCounters.set(key, 0);\\n      getRelayLoopGuard()?.reset();\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  // AB-7b: a real user message always preempts a pending goal\\n  // continuation. Runs after the restart ack so\\n          // if this throws the user still sees the \\\"✅\\\" message.\\n          // restartReason (C3) is propagated into the continuation prompt\\n          // so the post-restart first turn knows why it was restarted.\\n          await maybeAutoResumeGoalAfterRestart(\\n            marker.chatId,\\n            marker.userId,\\n            marker.reason,\\n          ).catch((err) => {\\n            console.error(\\n              `[restart] goal auto-resume for chat ${marker.chatId} failed:`,\\n              err,\\n            );\\n          });\\n        }\\n      } catch (err: any) {\\n        if (err?.code !== \\\"ENOENT\\\") {\\n          console.error(\\\"[restart] post-restart notify failed:\\\", err);\\n        }\\n      }\\n    },\\n  });\\n}\\n\\nconst shutdown = async (signal: string) => {\\n  console.log(`\\\\nReceived ${signal}, shutting down…`);\\n  try {\\n    scheduler.stop();\\n  } catch {\\n    /* noop */\\n  }\\n  try {\\n    await bot.stop();\\n  } catch {\\n    /* noop */\\n  }\\n  try {\\n    await withTimeout(\\n      sessions.shutdown(),\\n      config.memoryExtractTimeoutMs + 30_000,\\n      \\\"sessions.shutdown\\\",\\n    );\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    console.error(\\\"[shutdown] graceful shutdown failed:\\\", err);\\n  }\\n  try { stopPetApp(); } catch { /* noop */ }\\n  process.exit(0);\\n};\\n\\nprocess.on(\\\"SIGINT\\\", () => shutdown(\\\"SIGINT\\\"));\\nprocess.on(\\\"SIGTERM\\\", () => shutdown(\\\"SIGTERM\\\"));\\n\\n// Last-resort crash handlers: log and exit non-zero so the outer\\n// start.bat loop (or any supervisor) restarts us.\\nprocess.on(\\\"uncaughtException\\\", (err) => {\\n  console.error(\\\"uncaughtException:\\\", err);\\n  process.exit(1);\\n});\\nprocess.on(\\\"unhandledRejection\\\", (reason) => {\\n  console.error(\\\"unhandledRejection:\\\", reason);\\n  process.exit(1);\\n});\\n\\nmain().catch((err) => {\\n  console.error(\\\"Fatal:\\\", err);\\n  process.exit(1);\\n});\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[{\"oldStart\":262,\"oldLines\":6,\"newStart\":262,\"newLines\":12,\"lines\":[\"     return;\",\"   }\",\" \",\"+  if (kind === \\\"DT\\\") {\",\"+    // Dream todo：報告按鈕點擊 → 把 STATE.md High Priority 丟給主 session\",\"+    await handleDreamTodoCallback(ctx);\",\"+    return;\",\"+  }\",\"+\",\"   if (kind === \\\"A\\\") {\",\"     // ASK callback: data is `A:{question_id}:{key}`. split(\\\":\\\", 2) above\",\"     // only gave us the first two fields, so re-split here to get the key.\"]}],\"userModified\":false,\"replaceAll\":false}"</outcome>

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Dream callback routing integration completed</title>
  <subtitle>Added DT callback dispatcher branch to route dream suggestion button taps to handleDreamTodoCallback handler</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>index.ts callback_query dispatcher now includes "DT" kind branch routing to handleDreamTodoCallback</fact>
    <fact>Routing branch positioned after "SS" (skillsearch) and before "A" (ASK) in dispatcher sequence</fact>
    <fact>Comment documents purpose: "Dream todo：報告按鈕點擊 → 把 STATE.md High Priority 丟給主 session"</fact>
    <fact>Handler invoked with only ctx parameter, no data parameter needed since handler re-reads STATE.md</fact>
    <fact>Integration completes full A+D flow: preamble injection (sessionManager) + button notification (dream command) + callback handling (dispatcher)</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer completed the callback routing integration by adding a "DT" branch to the callback_query dispatcher in index.ts. When the inline keyboard sends callback_data "DT:hp" (from the "▶️ 處理 High Priority" button added earlier), the dispatcher now routes it to handleDreamTodoCallback. The routing follows the existing pattern established by "IL" (intel) and "SS" (skillsearch) callbacks, detecting the kind prefix and delegating to the appropriate handler. The handler doesn't need the data parameter because it re-reads STATE.md at callback time to ensure always-current data. This completes the full implementation of both Option A (automatic preamble injection making all new sessions aware of pending suggestions via the frozen preamble block) and Option D (interactive buttons allowing users to tap a button on the dream report to immediately trigger suggestion processing). The end-to-end flow now works: /dream writes STATE.md → sends button notification if High Priority exists → user taps button → dispatcher routes to handler → handler reads current STATE.md → constructs agent prompt → invokes runPrompt to process suggestions.</narrative>

The implementation is now complete and ready for runtime deployment: new agent sessions will automatically receive pending dream suggestions in their preamble (Option A, working across all ACP adapters), and /dream reports will include an interactive button allowing users to immediately trigger suggestion processing (Option D). The 48-hour freshness gate prevents stale suggestions from being injected, and the callback handler re-reads STATE.md at tap time to ensure always-current data. All smoke tests passed with production STATE.md data showing correct parsing of 3 real High Priority items from the July 6, 2026 dream run.</narrative>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Production STATE.md structure validation</title>
  <subtitle>Confirmed STATE.md format matches parser expectations with proper three-tier hierarchy and dash-list formatting</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>STATE.md follows strict format: title header, Last run timestamp line, three-tier sections (High Priority, Watch List, Noise)</fact>
    <fact>High Priority section contains 3 actionable items: skill-usage store split, orphaned wiki skill, uncommitted working tree changes</fact>
    <fact>Watch List section contains 8 monitoring items covering log integrity, documentation updates, skill redundancy, and technical debt</fact>
    <fact>All items use dash-list format ("- item text") matching parser's extraction pattern</fact>
    <fact>No placeholder "— 無" entries present in current state, only substantive items</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The developer examined the production STATE.md file to validate the format matches the parser's expectations. The file demonstrates the three-tier Loop State structure established by the dream command: a title line, Last run timestamp, High Priority section with 3 items requiring human intervention, and Watch List section with 8 items for monitoring. The format strictly follows the dream.ts statePrompt requirements: each section uses dash-list entries, items are concise single-line descriptions, and no placeholder "— 無" entries are present when sections have content. This validates the parser implementation against real-world data: the regex pattern /^## High Priority[^\n]*\n([\s\S]*?)(?=^## |$)/ correctly captures content between the High Priority heading and the next ## heading (Watch List), the filter logic successfully extracts dash-list items, and the timestamp parsing handles ISO format correctly. The absence of placeholder entries in this snapshot confirms the dream routine only populates sections with actual findings, leaving empty sections to show "— 無" as documented. This real-world validation demonstrates the full A+D implementation will function correctly with the existing dream workflow's output format.</narrative>
